column2: size 0x6000) so existing WiFi creds + the admin PIN are

column2: the required size) records which OTA

column1: #     slot is active and the rollback/validation state.
#   * Two equal app slots (ota_0 / ota_1) enable A/B updates with rollback.
#     6MB each is 4x the current ~1.5MB display image — generous headroom for
#     UI growth while leaving ~3.8MB of flash free at the top (ends at 0xC20000
#     well within the 16MB / 0x1000000 device).
#   * App slots are 64KB-aligned (a hard requirement for app partitions on the
#     ESP32-S3 because of the MMU flash-mapping granularity); otadata/phy are
#     4KB-aligned (the flash sector size).
#   * prompts (added for the media beachhead): a raw data partition carved out of
#     the previously-unpartitioned top-of-flash. ota_1 ends at 0xC20000; the 16MB
#     (0x1000000) device leaves 0x3E0000 (~3.87MB) free above it. Reserved now —
#     while OTA slots are still byte-identical — for FUTURE stored prompts / voicemail
#     (recorded G.711 µ-law clips). The synthesized-tone beachhead does NOT use it.
#     Subtype 0x40 = an undefined/raw data partition: we store fixed-layout raw µ-law
#     frames addressed by esp_partition_read/write rather than a filesystem. Rationale
#     (see docs/OTA.md): raw avoids the SPIFFS/LittleFS RAM + code overhead and wear-
#     leveling indirection on a constrained node
#     append-mostly with a known frame size
column2: so a filesystem buys little. If random-

column1: #     access named-file management is later needed
column2: reformat this same region as SPIFFS

column1: #     (subtype 0x82) WITHOUT moving the app slots — the OTA layout stays compatible.
